topicData.topic: tcs and anthropic , new deal for giving associates AI access
topicData.angle: tcs and anthropic , new deal for giving associates AI access

postText:
@TCS just signed a deal with @Anthropic to give their associates AI access.

600,000 people. That is not a pilot.

I keep thinking about what that actually means on the ground. Not the press release version. The real version. A delivery manager in Pune opening Claude for the first time, not totally sure what to ask it.

That moment is where this either works or it doesn't.

Most big AI rollouts I've seen talked about end up as shelfware. Not because the tool is bad. Because nobody changed the work around it. The tool just sits next to the old way of doing things.

TCS is betting that scale alone creates the shift. Maybe it does. 600,000 people experimenting is a lot of surface area for something useful to emerge.

I genuinely don't know if this one lands differently. But I'm curious what the associates actually end up using it for, not what the deck says they will.

If you're inside TCS right now, what does day one of this actually look like?

#AI #BuildingWithAI #TechTakes
